title: Engineering products that scale, teams that deliver, and platforms that last.
description: My career has been built around solving difficult engineering problems across backend platforms, Android applications, distributed systems and payment infrastructure. I enjoy working where architecture, scalability, product strategy and engineering execution intersect.
who_i_am_paragraphs: I'm a software engineer with more than 14 years of professional experience building cloud-native platforms, Android applications, distributed systems and payment products.

Throughout my career I've worked from low-level Android development to backend architecture, designing highly available services, event-driven systems, secure authentication mechanisms and resilient APIs used by millions of customers.

My experience spans technical leadership, architecture definition, performance optimization, cloud migration, developer experience, observability, reliability engineering and security-first software design.

While many engineers specialize exclusively in either backend or mobile, I've spent years building both. That dual perspective allows me to design end-to-end solutions, understanding every layer from Android UX to distributed microservices, payment orchestration and cloud infrastructure.

strengths.title: Android Engineering
strengths.description: Kotlin, Java, MVVM, MVI, Jetpack Compose, security, Wallet, NFC, performance.

strengths.title: Leadership
strengths.description: Technical strategy, architecture reviews, mentoring, engineering excellence.

strengths.title: Payments
strengths.description: Pix, Open Finance, NFC, Samsung Wallet, Visa, Mastercard.

philosophy.title: Technology should enable business, not become its bottleneck.

items.title: Architecture before implementation
items.description: Strong systems begin with clear boundaries, scalable abstractions, well-defined ownership and operational simplicity.

items.title: Developer Experience matters
items.description: Great engineering organizations build internal platforms, automation, documentation and tooling that allow developers to move quickly without sacrificing quality.

items.title: Reliability is a feature
items.description: Observability, resiliency, retries, idempotency, fault isolation and graceful degradation should be designed from day one.

items.title: Leadership through influence
items.description: The best technical leaders multiply the effectiveness of the people around them, creating environments where teams can build confidently and continuously improve.
